rulings.Case Title: ANA MACDONALD VS. DUKE PARTNERS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set Two And Requests For Productions Of Documents Set Two From Defendant Nationstar Mortgage, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Friday December 23, 2019 Line 1. PLAINTIFF ANA MACDONALD, LUELLA BROWN Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set Two And Requests For Productions Of Documents Set Two From Defendant Nationstar Mortgage, Llc is Granted in part and Denied in part. Motion is granted as to Special Interrogatories 26, 28, 29, 30 and Requests for Production 35, 36, 42, 43, 44, 45, 46, 47, 49. Motion is DENIED as to Requests for Production 52, 53, 59, 60, 61, 63, 64, 66 and 67. Responses to be served within 2 weeks of notice of entry of order. Requests for sanctions are denied. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Mandatory Relief From Order Striking Answer And Entering Default And Entry Of Default Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Per Ccp 473b
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Friday December 23, 2019 Line . DEFENDANT XELAN PROP 1, LLC. Motion For Mandatory Relief From Order Striking Answer And Entering Default And Entry Of Default Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Per Ccp 473b is GRANTED. Counsel for Xelan Prop 1. LLC moves for mandatory relief under CCP 473(b) apparently for her failure to bring to the Court's attention at the Monday, September 9, 2019 hearing the fact that she has submitted a Substitution of Attorney for filing at 9:24 p.m. on Friday, September 6, 2019. Counsel explains that she believed that the Motion to Strike Answer (based on lack of representation for a corporate defendant) calendared for September 9, 2019 had been taken off calendar on August 27, 2019 by virtue of service of the Notice of Taking off Calendar of a Motion for and Order Deeming Admissions Admitted, Terminating Sanctions, Striking Defendants Answer (based on the provisions of the Discovery Act and failure to comply with discovery) purportedly calendared for September 17, 2019. Counsel does not explain how she mistook one motion for another considering different grounds for relief and different hearing dates for the motions. It is also not explained by counsel why she did not inquire further regarding September 9, 2019 hearing having received an e-mail from opposing counsel on September 6, 2019 stating: "The Court issued a tentative ruling on Friday granting the Bank's motion to strike XELAN's Answer that is set for hearing on Monday at 9:30 a.m, which was not disputed. If you have an issue with that, you can appear and make your position known to the Court." However, the Court will grant this motion for relief because, had counsel appeared at the hearing to alert the Court of the filing of the Substitution of Attorney, the motion to strike would not have been granted. =(501/CFH)
